name: Pentagon Attack
description: The Pentagon attack occurred on September 11, 2001, when American Airlines Flight 77 crashed into the western side of the Pentagon in Arlington, Virginia, as part of the larger coordinated terrorist attacks on the United States. Official investigations, including the 9/11 Commission Report, concluded that the attack was perpetrated by al-Qaeda terrorists who hijacked the plane and deliberately crashed it into the building. The impact caused a partial collapse of the Pentagon's west side and resulted in 184 fatalities (125 in the Pentagon and 59 on the plane). Conspiracy theories surrounding the Pentagon attack allege that the official narrative is false. Proponents often claim that a missile, rather than a Boeing 757, struck the Pentagon, citing the lack of readily visible plane debris, the size of the hole in the building, and witness testimonies. They argue that the government orchestrated or allowed the attack to occur as a pretext for war in the Middle East. Mainstream scholars and investigators have thoroughly debunked these claims, providing evidence such as recovered plane parts, black box recordings, eyewitness accounts confirming a plane impact, and structural engineering analysis that explains the nature of the damage. The lack of extensive debris is attributed to the high-speed impact and subsequent fire, which consumed much of the aircraft. The size of the hole is consistent with the impact of a Boeing 757, considering the angle of impact and the building's structural design. The conspiracy theories surrounding the Pentagon attack are widely considered to be unsubstantiated and harmful, as they perpetuate misinformation and disrespect the victims and their families.
subtitle: Conspiracy theories surrounding the 9/11 attack

claims.claim: A missile, not a plane, hit the Pentagon.
claims.evidence: Lack of readily visible plane debris, small size of the hole in the building, alleged witness testimonies claiming to have seen a missile.
claims.criticism: Recovered plane parts (including the black boxes) confirm it was Flight 77. The size of the hole is consistent with a Boeing 757's impact, considering the angle and structural collapse. Eyewitness accounts overwhelmingly support the plane impact.

claims.claim: The government orchestrated or allowed the attack to happen.
claims.evidence: Alleged inconsistencies in the official narrative, perceived motives for war in the Middle East, perceived lack of adequate defense of US airspace.
claims.criticism: The 9/11 Commission Report and other investigations provide a detailed and consistent account of the events. The 'stand down' order is a misinterpretation of standard operating procedures. There is no credible evidence to support government involvement.

claims.claim: The damage was too minimal for a Boeing 757 impact.
claims.evidence: Photographs of the Pentagon immediately after the attack showing a relatively small hole.
claims.criticism: The initial hole was relatively small, but the impact caused a progressive collapse of the internal structure. The damage extended much further inside the building. The heat from the fire also weakened the structure.

keyFigures.name: Thierry Meyssan
keyFigures.role: Author of '9/11: The Big Lie,' a prominent conspiracy book.

keyFigures.name: David Ray Griffin
keyFigures.role: Author and theologian who promoted 9/11 conspiracy theories.

keyFigures.name: David Dunbar
keyFigures.role: Co-author of 'Debunking 9/11 Myths,' which refutes conspiracy theories.

timeline.event: American Airlines Flight 77 crashes into the Pentagon.
Thierry Meyssan publishes '9/11: The Big Lie'.
Publication of 'Debunking 9/11 Myths: Why Conspiracy Theories Can't Stand Up to the Facts'

sources.author: David Dunbar and Brad Reagan
sources.work: Debunking 9/11 Myths: Why Conspiracy Theories Can't Stand Up to the Facts
sources.title: David Dunbar and Brad Reagan
